channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Ultra-coherent meta-emitter tailors arbitrary thermal wavefront
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72460-2

Author Correction: Ultra-coherent meta-emitter tailors arbitrary thermal wavefront
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Ultra-coherent meta-emitter tailors arbitrary thermal wavefront

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72460-2 Author Correction: Ultra-coherent meta-emitter tailors arbitrary thermal wavefront

item.title: Photonic computing enabled end-to-end image compression and reconstruction with adjustable ratio and high-fidelity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71296-0

Researchers developed an optical computing system for adjustable image compression and high-quality reconstruction. Outperforming GPUs in speed and efficiency, it provides a scalable solution for high-speed, real-time image signal processing.
item.dc:title: Photonic computing enabled end-to-end image compression and reconstruction with adjustable ratio and high-fidelity
item.dc:creator: Yuhang Wang, Ang Li, Yihang Shao, Qiang Li, Yang Zhao, Shilong Pan
Ang Li

Shilong Pan

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE_UNCLASSIFIED

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71296-0 Researchers developed an optical computing system for adjustable image compression and high-quality reconstruction. Outperforming GPUs in speed and efficiency, it provides a scalable solution for high-speed, real-time image signal processing.

item.title: Spurious alignment between large language models and brains can emerge from non-robust methods and overlooked confounds
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72253-7

Whether large language models (LLMs) process language in a human-like manner remains unclear. Here, the authors re-examine prior LLM-to-brain mapping results, demonstrating that LLM-brain alignment can result from non-robust methods.
item.dc:title: Spurious alignment between large language models and brains can emerge from non-robust methods and overlooked confounds
item.dc:creator: Nima Hadidi, Ebrahim Feghhi, Bryan H. Song, Idan A. Blank, Jonathan C. Kao
Ebrahim Feghhi
Bryan H. Song
Idan A. Blank
Jonathan C. Kao

Authors: Nima Hadidi, Ebrahim Feghhi, Bryan H. Song, Idan A. Blank, Jonathan C. Kao

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Idan A. Blank, Jonathan C. Kao

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE_UNCLASSIFIED

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72253-7 Whether large language models (LLMs) process language in a human-like manner remains unclear. Here, the authors re-examine prior LLM-to-brain mapping results, demonstrating that LLM-brain alignment can result from non-robust methods.

item.title: The history and function of a circular RNA
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71822-0

In this paper, the authors review how the circular RNA Cdr1as and its interacting non-coding RNAs regulate neuronal stress responses and synaptic activity, highlighting general principles of RNA-based regulatory mechanisms and their relevance for brain function, disease, and therapy.
item.dc:title: The history and function of a circular RNA
item.dc:creator: Cledi A. Cerda-Jara, Flavia Scoyni, Grygoriy Zolotarov, et al.; last authors: Jørgen Kjems, Nikolaus Rajewsky

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71822-0 In this paper, the authors review how the circular RNA Cdr1as and its interacting non-coding RNAs regulate neuronal stress responses and synaptic activity, highlighting general principles of RNA-based regulatory mechanisms and their relevance for brain function, disease, and therapy.

item.title: Coherent control through phonon anharmonicity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71891-1

Double pump-probe tracks the Raman phonon frequency vs time in SnTe/SnSe, separating electronic, heating, and other anharmonic effects and reveals emergent anharmonicity. Mode specific electron phonon couplings are extracted.
item.dc:title: Coherent control through phonon anharmonicity
item.dc:creator: Gili Scharf, Tomer Hasharoni, Lara Donval, Leah Ben Gur, Alon Ron
Tomer Hasharoni
Lara Donval
Leah Ben Gur
Alon Ron
item.description:
Authors: Gili Scharf, Tomer Hasharoni, Lara Donval, Leah Ben Gur, Alon Ron

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Leah Ben Gur, Alon Ron

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE_UNCLASSIFIED

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Tags: #phonon

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71891-1 Double pump-probe tracks the Raman phonon frequency vs time in SnTe/SnSe, separating electronic, heating, and other anharmonic effects and reveals emergent anharmonicity. Mode specific electron phonon couplings are extracted.

item.title: Broad spectrum structure discovery in large-scale higher-order networks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71903-0

Identifying higher-order structure that governs how units interact is crucial for modeling complex systems. Here, authors develop a class of scalable probabilistic models that efficiently extract a spectrum of assortative, core-periphery and disassortative structure from higher-order network data.
item.dc:title: Broad spectrum structure discovery in large-scale higher-order networks
item.dc:creator: John Hood, Caterina De Bacco, Aaron Schein
Caterina De Bacco
Aaron Schein
item.description:
Authors: John Hood, Caterina De Bacco, Aaron Schein

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Caterina De Bacco, Aaron Schein

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE_UNCLASSIFIED

Why: Gemini failed; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71903-0 Identifying higher-order structure that governs how units interact is crucial for modeling complex systems. Here, authors develop a class of scalable probabilistic models that efficiently extract a spectrum of assortative, core-periphery and disassortative structure from higher-order network data.

item.title: Single-bacterium metabolome revealing heterogeneous cellular states in bacterial populations
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72373-0

Here the authors present a single-bacterium metabolomics platform that enables sensitive metabolic profiling of individual bacteria, revealing metabolic heterogeneity in heteroresistant K. pneumoniae strains and providing insights into bacterial survival and resistance.
item.dc:title: Single-bacterium metabolome revealing heterogeneous cellular states in bacterial populations

item.title: Author Correction: Dolutegravir restores gut microbiota in late-stage HIV-1 unlike darunavir: an open-label, randomized clinical trial

Nature Communications, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72458-w

Author Correction: Dolutegravir restores gut microbiota in late-stage HIV-1 unlike darunavir: an open-label, randomized clinical trial
